last_item: Band-1.5: surface restore-verification status at a glance — restoreTestStatus classifier + RestoreTestStatus banner on Backups.tsx, covered by vitest/RTL
last_result: done — the Backups page already listed restore-test history in a table but nothing answered 'have my backups ever been proven recoverable?' up front. Added exported pure restoreTestStatus(tests) → never|passed|failed|never-passed union (mirrors backupFreshness, reuses shared SUCCESS/FAILURE vocab) + a RestoreTestStatus banner above the Restore-tests card. The 'never' state is DELIBERATELY calm (info tone, no call-to-action) because execution is still a stub → only 'never' can occur in prod today, and the banner must not nudge toward a button that 500s; failed/never-passed shout danger for when execution lands. 9 vitest/RTL tests (classifier × all branches incl. unknown-latest; component × all four tones incl. verified-rows display). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking issues. Web typecheck/33 tests/build + full Go gate green.
notes: Band 1.5 (data durability) IN PROGRESS. Done: (1) 'last good backup was N ago' surfaced (Dashboard + Backups banner). (2) backup-failed/backup-stale rules + metrics + Collector.enrichBackup. (3) telemetry+alert evaluation LOOP wired (background.go) — alerts fire in prod. (4) ***SCHEDULED BACKUPS NOW RUN: full+incremental cron jobs registered in startBackgroundJobs; Manager.backupMu single-flight guard makes overlaps a CodeConflict skip (no fail row, no false alert).*** (5) off-host S3 nudge test-locked. (6) restore-test SURFACING done: restoreTestStatus + RestoreTestStatus on Backups.tsx + vitest/RTL. REMAINING 1.5: restore-test EXECUTION is the real next durability item and is now a DESIGN-FIRST backlog entry — handleRestoreTest is still a stub returning typed 'not implemented', so the surfacing can only show 'never' today. Approaches: pgbackrest verify (cheap, repo integrity, no restore) vs non-destructive scratch restore + pg_controldata vs full scratch restore + boot + row count. (b)/(c) need a disk-headroom precheck (restore ≈ DB size could fill the box) + guaranteed scratch cleanup; never touch live pg1-path; unit-test via fake Runner like Backup/Restore, deep proof integration-tagged. NO sm/DEFAULTS precedent — genuine design call, do NOT rush. Optionally wire OTLP exporter (nil in collector). Plumbing: alert engine reads Snapshot FIELDS via alert/metrics.go metricValue (separate key namespace from telemetry/snapshot.go MetricKeys). Backups: internal/backup/manager.go Backup() takes backupMu.TryLock first, then ownership, writes terminal 'fail'/'success'; internal/store/backup.go (ListBackups/ListRestoreTests newest-first, LatestSuccessfulBackup). Scheduler: internal/scheduler — empty spec disables (Register no-op); Jobs() lists registered. go/psql/pg_ctl snap-sandboxed (run sandbox-disabled). TMPDIR empty in some shells — use /tmp.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
